preamble: The shift from the voiced velar stop [ɡ] to the voiced uvular stop [ɢ] is a process of retraction. This backing often occurs under the influence of back vowels or emphatic consonants, or as part of a push-chain where new palatal sounds move the velars further back.

examples.note: Retraction of velar stops in emphatic environments.

sources: Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
